stem: Let \( f(x) = \begin{cases} kx + ([[b]]) & x < [[c]] \\ ([[d]])x^{2} + ([[e]]) & x \ge [[c]] \end{cases} \), where \( k \) is a constant. For what value of \( k \) is \( f \) continuous at \( x=[[c]] \)?
solution: Continuity at \( x=[[c]] \) requires the two pieces to agree there: \( k([[c]]) + ([[b]]) = ([[d]])([[c]])^{2} + ([[e]]) \). Solving for \( k \) gives \( k = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This confuses the required VALUE at \( x=c \) (the quadratic piece) with \( k \) itself.
This forgets to subtract \( b \) before dividing by \( c \), effectively dropping it with the wrong sign.
This adds \( b \) instead of subtracting it before dividing by \( c \).
This is the derivative of the quadratic piece at \( x=c \), not the value needed for continuity.
This is off by one from the correct value.
This is off by one from the correct value.

stem: Let \( f(x) = \dfrac{[[a]]x^{2} - [[k]]}{x - [[r]]} \) for \( x \ne [[r]] \). For what value \( L \) should \( f([[r]]) \) be DEFINED so that \( f \) is continuous at \( x=[[r]] \)?
distractor_rationales: This forgets to double \( a \cdot r \), dropping the factor of 2 that comes from \( x+r\to 2r \).
This reports the removed factor \( k=ar^{2} \) itself, not the limit.
This forgets the factor of \( r \) entirely.
This mistakenly assumes the hole's limit is always 0.
This is off by one from the correct value.
This is off by one from the correct value.

stem: Let \( f(x) = [[a]](x - [[r]])^{3} \), which is continuous everywhere. Since \( f([[lo]]) < 0 < f([[hi]]) \) (or vice versa), the Intermediate Value Theorem guarantees a root of \( f \) in \( ([[lo]], [[hi]]) \). Find that root.
solution: \( f(x) = [[a]](x-[[r]])^{3} = 0 \) exactly when \( x=[[r]] \), which lies in \( ([[lo]], [[hi]]) \); this is the guaranteed root, \( x = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This is the left endpoint of the interval, not the root.
This is the right endpoint of the interval, not the root.
This is off by three from the interval's midpoint, matching the right endpoint by coincidence of construction.
This reports the leading coefficient instead of solving \( f(x)=0 \).
This is off by one from the correct value.
This is off by one from the correct value.

stem: Let \( f(x) = \begin{cases} a & x < [[c1]] \\ [[m]]x + b & [[c1]] \le x \le [[c2]] \\ [[n]]x & x > [[c2]] \end{cases} \), where \( a \) and \( b \) are constants chosen so \( f \) is continuous everywhere. What is \( a \)?
solution: Matching the last two pieces at \( x=[[c2]] \) gives \( b = ([[n]]-[[m]])([[c2]]) \). Matching the first two pieces at \( x=[[c1]] \) gives \( a = [[m]]([[c1]]) + b = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This forgets to add the constant \( b \) from the second piece, using only its slope contribution.
This uses the third piece's slope \( n \) at \( x=c_{1} \) instead of matching through the middle piece.
This evaluates the middle piece's linear part at \( x=c_{2}\) instead of \( x=c_{1} \), and drops \( b \).
This has a sign error while trying to combine both continuity conditions at once.
This is off by one from the correct value.
This is off by one from the correct value.

stem: Let \( f(x) = \dfrac{\sin([[a]]x)}{x} \) for \( x \ne 0 \). For what value \( L \) should \( f(0) \) be DEFINED so that \( f \) is continuous at \( x=0 \)?
solution: Using the standard limit \( \lim_{u\to 0}\frac{\sin u}{u}=1 \) with \( u=[[a]]x \): \( \displaystyle\lim_{x\to 0}\frac{\sin([[a]]x)}{x} = [[a]]\lim_{x\to 0}\frac{\sin([[a]]x)}{[[a]]x} = [[a]]\cdot 1 = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This mistakenly assumes the limit is always 0 because the numerator vanishes at \( x=0 \).
This forgets to scale by \( a \), using the bare standard limit value of 1.
This inverts the scaling factor, dividing by \( a \) instead of multiplying.
This squares the coefficient instead of leaving it linear.
This is off by one from the correct value.
This is off by one from the correct value.
